short: Muhammad (also Mohammad/Mohammed) is commonly interpreted as "praiseworthy."
long: Muhammad (also spelled Mohammad or Mohammed) is a widely used given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly understood to mean "praiseworthy" or "worthy of praise." Usage and transliterations vary across regions and languages.
notes: Multiple common transliterations.

short: Ahmed (also spelled Ahmad or Ahmet) is a given name of Arabic origin meaning "highly praised" or "most commendable."
long: Ahmed (also spelled Ahmad or Ahmet) is a given name of Arabic origin, derived from the root ḥ-m-d (praise). It is commonly interpreted as "highly praised," "most commendable," or "one who thanks God." In Islamic tradition, it is recognized as one of the names associated with the Prophet Muhammad and is often connected with gratitude and virtue.
notes: Mentioned in Quran 61:6.

short: Ali is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "elevated" or "exalted."
long: Ali is a widely used given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly understood to mean "elevated," "exalted," or "sublime." The name has deep roots in Arabic linguistic tradition and is used across many cultures and regions.

short: Yusuf (also Yousef) is a given name of Semitic origin, often associated with the meaning "God increases."
long: Yusuf (also spelled Yousef or Yousuf) is a given name of Semitic origin found across multiple linguistic traditions. It is commonly interpreted as "God increases" or "He will add." The name appears prominently in Quranic and Biblical narratives.
notes: Name of a Quranic and Biblical prophet.

short: Umar (also Omar) is a given name of Arabic origin, often associated with "flourishing" or "long-lived."
long: Umar (also spelled Omar) is a widely used given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly associated with meanings such as "flourishing," "long-lived," or "thriving." The name has been in use for centuries across many cultures.

short: Hamza is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "strong" or "steadfast."
long: Hamza is a given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly interpreted as "strong," "steadfast," or "brave." The meaning "lion" is also popularly attributed to the name, though this is a cultural association rather than a strict etymological derivation.

short: Abdullah is a given name of Arabic origin meaning "servant of God (Allah)."
long: Abdullah (also spelled Abdallah) is a given name of Arabic origin composed of two elements: "abd" (servant) and "Allah" (God). It is commonly understood to mean "servant of God" and is widely used across many cultures and regions.

short: Ibrahim (also Ibraheem) is a given name of Semitic origin, commonly associated with "father of many."
long: Ibrahim (also spelled Ibraheem) is a given name of Semitic origin found in both Arabic and Hebrew linguistic traditions. It is commonly interpreted as "father of many" or "father of nations." The name appears prominently in Quranic and Biblical narratives as the name of a major prophet.

short: Adam is a given name of Semitic origin, often associated with "earth" or "mankind."
long: Adam is a given name with deep roots in Semitic languages. It is commonly associated with meanings related to "earth," "ground," or "mankind." The name appears in many religious and cultural traditions as the name of the first human being.

short: Zayn (also Zain) is a given name of Arabic origin meaning "beauty" or "grace."

short: Hassan (also Hasan) is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "handsome" or "good."

short: Bilal is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "moisture" or "freshness."
long: Bilal is a given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly interpreted as "moisture," "freshness," or "water." The name is historically significant in Islamic tradition and is widely used across many cultures.

short: Salman is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "safe" or "secure."
long: Salman is a given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly interpreted as "safe," "secure," or "peaceful." The name is widely used across many cultures and has historical significance in Islamic tradition.

short: Zaid (also Zayd) is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "growth" or "abundance."

short: Ismail (also Ismael) is a given name of Semitic origin commonly meaning "God hears" or "God will hear."

short: Dawud (also Dawood) is a given name of Semitic origin commonly meaning "beloved."

short: Yahya is a given name of Semitic origin commonly meaning "God gives life" or "living."
long: Yahya is a given name of Semitic origin. It is commonly interpreted as "God gives life," "living," or "He lives." The name appears in the Quran as the name of a prophet, corresponding to John in Biblical tradition.
notes: Name of a Quranic prophet (John in Biblical tradition).

short: Idris is a given name of Arabic origin commonly associated with "studious" or "interpreter."
long: Idris is a given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly associated with meanings such as "studious," "interpreter," or "one who instructs." The name appears in the Quran as the name of an early prophet praised for patience and righteousness.

short: Musa is a given name of Semitic origin commonly meaning "drawn out" or "saved from water."
long: Musa is a given name of Semitic origin. Its meaning is often given as "drawn out" or "saved from water," though the exact etymology is debated across linguistic traditions. The name appears prominently in the Quran as one of the most frequently mentioned prophets.

short: Isa is a given name of Semitic origin, the Arabic form commonly associated with Jesus in Islamic tradition.
long: Isa is a given name of Semitic origin. It is the Arabic form of the name known as Jesus in English. The name appears prominently in the Quran as the name of a major prophet. Various etymologies have been proposed, though the exact original meaning is debated.

short: Luqman is a given name of Arabic origin commonly associated with wisdom.
long: Luqman is a given name of Arabic origin. It is most commonly associated with wisdom and sound counsel. The name appears in the Quran as Surah Luqman (31), where a wise figure advises his son on faith, gratitude, and good character.

short: Nuh is a given name of Semitic origin commonly meaning "rest" or "comfort."
long: Nuh is a given name of Semitic origin. It is commonly interpreted as "rest" or "comfort." The name appears in the Quran as the name of a major prophet known for building the ark, corresponding to Noah in Biblical tradition.

short: Daniyal is a given name of Semitic origin commonly meaning "God is my judge."

short: Salih (also Saleh) is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "virtuous" or "righteous."

short: Hud is a given name of Arabic origin, borne by a Quranic prophet.
long: Hud is a given name of Arabic origin. It appears in the Quran as the name of a prophet sent to the people of 'Ad. The exact etymological meaning is debated, though some scholars associate it with guidance or gentleness.

short: Usman (also Uthman/Osman) is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "baby bustard" or "devoted."

short: Abdurrahman is a given name of Arabic origin meaning "servant of the Most Merciful."

notes: Feminine form of Munir.

short: Nusaybah is a given name of Arabic origin, commonly associated with noble lineage.
long: Nusaybah is a given name of Arabic origin. Its exact etymological meaning is debated, though it is often associated with noble lineage or high origin. The name has deep historical significance in Islamic tradition and is widely used across many cultures.

short: Rahmah is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "mercy" or "compassion."
long: Rahmah (also spelled Rahma) is a given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly interpreted as "mercy," "compassion," or "grace." The concept of rahmah (divine mercy) is central in the Quran, and the name is widely used across many cultures.

short: Sawda is a given name of Arabic origin, often associated with a type of palm tree or dark complexion.
long: Sawda (also spelled Sawdah) is a given name of Arabic origin. Its meaning is sometimes given as referring to a type of palm tree, or associated with a dark or swarthy complexion. The name has historical significance in Islamic tradition and is used across various cultures.

short: Tasneem is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "a spring in Paradise."
long: Tasneem (also spelled Tasnim) is a given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly interpreted as referring to a spring or fountain in Paradise. The name appears in the Quran (Surah Al-Mutaffifin 83:27) and is widely used across many cultures.
notes: Mentioned in the Quran (83:27).

short: Wardah (also Warda) is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "rose" or "flower."
long: Wardah (also spelled Warda) is a given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly interpreted as "rose" or "flower." The name is widely used across many cultures and carries connotations of beauty and fragrance.

short: Ghada is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "graceful" or "delicate."
long: Ghada (also spelled Ghadah) is a given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly interpreted as "graceful," "delicate," or "beautiful young woman." The name is widely used across many cultures and regions.

short: Suha is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "a star" (referring to a faint star in Ursa Major).
long: Suha (also spelled Soha) is a given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly interpreted as referring to a small, faint star in the constellation Ursa Major (the Great Bear). In classical Arabic culture, being able to see this dim star was a sign of sharp eyesight.

short: Tala is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "gold" or "rising star."
long: Tala is a given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly interpreted as "gold," "rising star," or referring to a young palm tree. The name is concise and has gained popularity across many cultures in recent years.

short: Lina is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "tender" or "young palm tree."
long: Lina (also spelled Leena) is a given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly interpreted as "tender," "delicate," or "young palm tree." The name is used across many cultures and languages with slightly different connotations in each.

short: Maysa is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "one who walks with a proud gait."
long: Maysa (also spelled Maisa) is a given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly interpreted as "one who walks with a proud gait" or "graceful walker." The name is widely used across many cultures and carries connotations of elegance.

short: Abeer is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "fragrance" or "perfume."
long: Abeer (also spelled Abir) is a given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly interpreted as "fragrance," "perfume," or "scent of flowers." The name is widely used across many cultures and carries connotations of beauty and pleasantness.

short: Duha is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "forenoon" or "morning light."
long: Duha (also spelled Dhuha) is a given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly interpreted as "forenoon," "morning light," or "mid-morning brightness." The name is associated with Surah Ad-Duha (93) in the Quran and is widely used across many cultures.
notes: Associated with Surah Ad-Duha (93).

short: Jannah is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "garden" or "paradise."
long: Jannah (also spelled Jana or Janna) is a given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly interpreted as "garden" or "paradise." In Islamic theology, Jannah refers to the heavenly garden of Paradise. The name is widely used across many cultures.

short: Malaika is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "angels."
long: Malaika is a given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly interpreted as "angels" (the plural form of malak). The name is widely used across many cultures and carries connotations of purity and grace.

short: Rania is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "gazing" or "queenly."
long: Rania (also spelled Raniya or Ranya) is a given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly interpreted as "gazing," "content," or associated with queenly dignity. The name is widely used across many cultures and regions.

short: Shayma is a given name of Arabic origin commonly meaning "one with a beauty mark."
long: Shayma (also spelled Shaima or Shaymaa) is a given name of Arabic origin. It is commonly interpreted as "one with a beauty mark" or "one with a good nature." The name has historical significance in Islamic tradition and is widely used across many cultures.
